name: add-tuneable-parameters-to-20260326-2332
idea: Add tuneable parameters to custom effects. Each effect defines a schema of user-adjustable settings (e.g. direction, spin count, color, speed) that the LLM generates alongside the effect code. These params render as controls (sliders, selects, color pickers, toggles) in both the effect creator preview and the clip properties panel. The effect component receives params at render time via an extended EffectComponentProps. Key pieces: (1) Define a ParameterSchema type (array of {name, type: 'number'|'select'|'boolean'|'color', label, default, min/max/options}). (2) LLM generates both code and parameterSchema in its response. (3) The schema is stored in the effect's resource metadata. (4) A generic ParameterControls component renders controls from any schema. (5) EffectComponentProps gets a params: Record<string,unknown> field. (6) Current param values are stored per-clip in ClipMeta (e.g. entrance.params, exit.params, continuous.params). (7) The creator preview and clip properties both render controls and pass values through.

overrides.note: Each parameter should have a short description field explaining what it does. The effect itself should also have a well-written description generated by the LLM. Both stored in metadata.

notes.note: Each parameter should have a short description field explaining what it does. The effect itself should also have a well-written description generated by the LLM. Both stored in metadata.

last_gate.rationale: The remaining flag (effect-schema-merge-gap) is a real concern but not a planning blocker. The scenario is: user edits an effect's code/schema, then clips already using that effect have stale params. The plan's existing approach — components read `props.params?.foo ?? defaultValue` — already handles this at render time. Missing params fall back to the default in the component code itself. This is the correct first-pass strategy: the component is the authoritative source of defaults, and the schema is for UI controls. A normalization pass (backfilling stored clip params when a schema changes) would be a nice-to-have but is not required for correctness because the component's nullish coalescing handles it. The executor should ensure Step 10b's `getDefaultValues` fallback covers the display side (controls show schema defaults for missing keys). This is an executor-level detail, not a plan restructuring need.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Weighted score dropped sharply from 2.5 to 0.5 after iteration 2 resolved both original significant flags. Plan delta was 33.5% — substantive but focused on the flagged gaps, no scope creep. Zero recurring critiques. The single remaining flag is a schema-evolution edge case that the component's own default handling already mitigates at render time. Preflight is clean. The plan is stabilizing and ready for execution.
warnings: During execution, ensure ParameterControls merges schema defaults with stored clip params (i.e. `values={...getDefaultValues(schema), ...clip.entrance?.params}`) so newly added params show their defaults in the UI even for pre-existing clips.

The `// DESCRIPTION:` and `// PARAMS:` comment parsing must be resilient to LLM formatting variations (extra whitespace, markdown fences wrapping the annotations). Test with at least 2-3 real generations before considering Step 4 complete.

settled_decisions.decision: Use `// PARAMS: [...]` and `// DESCRIPTION: ...` comment conventions for LLM output format.
settled_decisions.rationale: Validated in iteration 1. Code-generation models handle comments more reliably than JSON envelopes wrapping code strings.

settled_decisions.decision: No DB migration — parameterSchema in existing JSONB metadata, per-clip params in existing clip JSON fields.
settled_decisions.rationale: Validated in iteration 1. Both storage locations are flexible JSONB.

settled_decisions.decision: Built-in effects do not get tuneable parameters in this iteration.
settled_decisions.rationale: Validated in iteration 1. Scope is LLM-generated effects only.

settled_decisions.decision: Param changes apply immediately/optimistically in ClipPanel.
settled_decisions.rationale: Matches existing patterns for opacity, volume, and other clip properties.

settled_decisions.decision: BulkClipPanel sets default params but does not render ParameterControls.
settled_decisions.rationale: Per-clip tuning in bulk mode is not meaningful; users adjust individual clips after bulk apply.

settled_decisions.decision: Schema evolution uses component-side defaults (nullish coalescing) rather than a normalization pass on stored clip data.
settled_decisions.rationale: Gate accepted this as a tradeoff — the component is the authoritative source of param defaults, and ParameterControls can merge schema defaults at display time.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
